input.description: Explore unsloth project structure and save/export methods
input.prompt:
Explore the unsloth codebase structure. I need to understand:

1. The overall directory/file layout (top-level and subdirectories)
2. Where existing `save` and `export` methods are defined (search for method names like `save_model`, `save_pretrained`, `export_to_*`)
3. How the main model classes are structured — which files define the trainable model wrappers
4. Any existing ONNX-related code

Please be thorough. List all top-level files and directories, then drill into the most relevant files. Show me the full content (or large excerpts) of any file that defines save/export methods.

Be very thorough — "very thorough" level exploration.

input.description: Explore LoRA adapter structure and weights
input.prompt:
Explore the unsloth codebase focusing specifically on LoRA adapter weights — the A and B matrices.

I need to understand:
1. How LoRA layers are structured (what classes, what attributes hold A and B matrices)
2. Where `lora_A`, `lora_B`, `lora_alpha`, `r` (rank), and `target_modules` are stored/accessed
3. Which files contain LoRA-specific code
4. How the model iterates over its LoRA layers — any existing loops that traverse all LoRA modules
5. What metadata is tracked per LoRA layer (rank, alpha, target module names)

Search for patterns like `lora_A`, `lora_B`, `lora_alpha`, `r =`, `target_modules`, `LoraLayer`, `LinearLayer`.

Be very thorough in your exploration.

input.description: Explore unsloth model classes and method exposure patterns
input.prompt:
Explore the unsloth codebase focusing on:

1. How methods are exposed on the model objects — specifically how save/export methods get attached to the model (are they monkey-patched, added via mixins, defined in __init__, etc.)
2. The `FastLanguageModel` class or similar entry-point classes — how they wrap the underlying model
3. Any `__init__.py` files that export the public API
4. Look for patterns like `model.save_pretrained`, `model.push_to_hub`, `model.export_*` — how these are attached to model objects
5. Any `get_peft_model` or similar calls that set up LoRA

Search for these patterns thoroughly and show me the relevant code sections with file paths and line numbers.

content.text:
Explore the unsloth codebase focusing on:

1. How methods are exposed on the model objects — specifically how save/export methods get attached to the model (are they monkey-patched, added via mixins, defined in __init__, etc.)

5. Any `get_peft_model` or similar calls that set up LoRA

Search for these patterns thoroughly and show me the relevant code sections with file paths and line numbers.

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass
364

366	def apply_lora_qkv(self, X, inplace = True):

368	    QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS = get_lora_parameters(self.q_proj)
369	    KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS = get_lora_parameters(self.k_proj)
370	    VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS = get_lora_parameters(self.v_proj)
371	    Q, K, V = LoRA_QKV.apply(X,
372	        QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
373	        KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
374	        VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
375	        inplace,
376	    )
377	    return Q, K, V

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass
458

463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O
465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530

67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.

268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:

295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats

300	        device_index = device.index

303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()

309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS

311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)

321	            out_absmax = ABSMAX_BUFFER[:n_elements_absmax]

327	                assert(out.dtype == dtype)
328	            out_absmax = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch_float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
329	        pass

332	        ptr_out_absmax = get_ptr(out_absmax)
333	        with torch_gpu_device(device):
334	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32(
335	                get_ptr(code2), get_ptr(absmax), get_ptr(absmax2), ptr_out_absmax,
336	                ctypes_c_int(blocksize2), ctypes_c_int(n_elements_absmax), XPU_STREAM
337	            )

344	               ctypes_c_int(blocksize), ctypes_c_int(out.numel()), XPU_STREAM,)

347	        is_transposed = (True if W.shape[0] == 1 else False)

691	def fast_linear_forward(proj, X, temp_lora = None, out = None):
692	
693	    W, W_quant, lora_A, lora_B, lora_S, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)
694	    bsz, q_len, in_dim = X.shape
695	    if q_len != 1: return matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, lora_A, lora_B, lora_S)
696	
697	    if W_quant is None:
698	        out = torch_matmul(X, W.t(), out = out)
699	    elif bsz == 1 and q_len == 1:
700	        out = fast_gemv(X, W, W_quant, out = out)
701	    else:
702	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)
703	        out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
704	    pass
705	
706	    # Add in LoRA weights
707	    if lora_A is not None:
708	        out_dim = out.shape[2]
709	        dtype = X.dtype
710	
711	        if not hasattr(lora_A, "_fast_lora"):
712	            lora_A._fast_lora = lora_A.to(dtype)
713	            lora_B._fast_lora = lora_B.to(dtype)
714	        pass
715	
716	        if bsz == 1:
717	            out = out.view(out_dim)
718	            temp_lora = torch_mv(lora_A._fast_lora, X.ravel(), out = temp_lora)
719	            out.addmv_(lora_B._fast_lora, temp_lora, alpha = lora_S)
720	        else:
721	            out = out.view(bsz, out_dim)
722	            temp_lora = torch_mm(X.view(bsz, in_dim), lora_A._fast_lora.t(), out = temp_lora)
723	            out.addmm_(temp_lora, lora_B._fast_lora.t(), alpha = lora_S)
724	        pass
725	        out = out.view(bsz, 1, out_dim)
726	    pass
727	
728	    if bias is not None: out += bias
729	
730	    return out
731	pass
732	
733	
734	def matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out = None):
735	    dtype = X.dtype
736	    W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)
737	
738	    if X.dim() == 3:
739	        batch, seq_len, d = X.shape
740	        X = X.view(-1, X.shape[-1])
741	        reshape = True
742	    else:
743	        reshape = False
744	    pass
745	    out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
746	    if W_quant is not None: del W
747	
748	    if A is not None:
749	        # LoRA is enabled
750	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
751	        XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
752	        out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha = s)
753	        # out += (X @ A.to(dtype)) @ (s * B.to(dtype))
754	    pass
755	
756	    return out.view(batch, seq_len, -1) if reshape else out
757	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass
213	
214	
215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

1643	def _prepare_model_for_qat(model: torch.nn.Module, qat_scheme: str) -> torch.nn.Module:
1644	    """
1645	    Transform a model for Quantization-Aware Training (QAT) during fine-tuning.
1646	
1647	    On a high level, this means fake quantizing the base (frozen) model during training.
1648	    Fake quantization refers to simulating quantization numerics in high precision (e.g. bf16).
1649	    This helps mitigate quantization degradations when the model is quantized after training.
1650	
1651	    QAT can be optionally combined with LoRA fine-tuning to for additional throughput improvement.
1652	    For more details: https://dev-discuss.pytorch.org/t/speeding-up-qat-by-1-89x-with-lora/2700
1653	    """
1654	    from torchao.quantization import (
1655	        Float8DynamicActivationInt4WeightConfig,
1656	        Float8DynamicActivationFloat8WeightConfig,
1657	        Int8DynamicActivationInt4WeightConfig,
1658	        Int4WeightOnlyConfig,
1659	        PerRow,
1660	        quantize_,
1661	    )
1662	    from torchao.quantization.qat import QATConfig
1663	    filter_fn = None
1664	    if qat_scheme == "fp8-int4":
1665	        group_size = 128
1666	        base_config = Float8DynamicActivationInt4WeightConfig()
1667	        filter_fn = lambda m, _: isinstance(m, torch.nn.Linear) and m.in_features >= group_size
1668	    elif qat_scheme == "fp8-fp8":
1669	        base_config = Float8DynamicActivationFloat8WeightConfig(granularity=PerRow())
1670	    elif qat_scheme == "int8-int4":
1671	        group_size = 32
1672	        base_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt4WeightConfig(group_size=group_size)
1673	        filter_fn = lambda m, _: isinstance(m, torch.nn.Linear) and m.in_features >= group_size
1674	    elif qat_scheme == "int4":
1675	        group_size = 128
1676	        base_config = Int4WeightOnlyConfig(group_size=group_size)
1677	        filter_fn = lambda m, _: isinstance(m, torch.nn.Linear) and m.in_features >= group_size
1678	    else:
1679	        raise ValueError(f"Unexpected QAT scheme {qat_scheme}")
1680	    pass
1681	    quantize_(model, QATConfig(base_config, step="prepare"), filter_fn=filter_fn)
1682	    return model

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive view. Let me create a thorough summary document:

## Summary: Unsloth Codebase Structure Exploration

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY AND FILE LAYOUT

**Root Directory Files:**
- `.git`, `.github/`, `.gitignore` - Git configuration
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `LICENSE`, `README.md` - Documentation
- `pyproject.toml` - Project configuration
- `unsloth-cli.py` - Command-line interface
- `images/` - Image assets
- `tests/` - Test suite
- `unsloth/` - Main package

**Unsloth Package Structure:**
```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                    # Main package initialization, imports all modules
├── _auto_install.py               # Auto-installation utilities
├── import_fixes.py                # Import-time patches for dependencies
├── chat_templates.py              # Chat template management (129KB)
├── save.py                        # Save/export core functionality (105KB) - CRITICAL
├── tokenizer_utils.py             # Tokenizer utilities (41KB)
├── trainer.py                     # Trainer patches (8.5KB)
├── models/                        # Model implementations
├── kernels/                       # CUDA/custom kernels
├── registry/                      # Model registry
├── dataprep/                      # Data preparation utilities
└── utils/                         # Utility functions
```

---

### 2. SAVE AND EXPORT METHODS LOCATION

**Primary Location: `/unsloth/save.py` (105KB file)**

This is the central hub for all save/export functionality. Key functions:

#### Core Saving Functions:
1. **`unsloth_save_model()`** (line 205-762)
   - Main entry point for saving models
   - Handles three save methods: `lora`, `merged_16bit`, `merged_4bit`
   - Supports push to HuggingFace Hub
   - Parameters: model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method, push_to_hub, token, etc.

2. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()`** (line 1321-1361)
   - Instance method added to models via `patch_saving_functions()`
   - Converts 4-bit weights to float16 with minimal overhead
   - Calls `unsloth_save_model()` internally

3. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()`** (line 1364-1407)
   - Instance method for pushing merged models to HuggingFace Hub
   - Converts 4-bit to float16 before pushing
   - Delegates to `unsloth_save_model()`

#### GGUF Conversion Functions:
4. **`save_to_gguf()`** (line 952-1320)
   - Main GGUF conversion function
   - Handles llama.cpp installation
   - Supports multiple quantization methods: `f32`, `f16`, `bf16`, `q8_0`, `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, etc.
   - Returns GGUF file locations

5. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()`** (line 1768-1948)
   - Instance method for saving as GGUF
   - Auto-converts 4-bit to float16, then to GGUF
   - Creates Ollama Modelfile
   - Optional push to HuggingFace Hub

6. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()`** (line 1951-2087)
   - Instance method for pushing GGUF to Hub
   - Similar to `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` but with push enabled

#### GGML (LoRA) Functions:
7. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()`** (line 2211-2262)
   - Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format (for llama.cpp)
   - Saves locally

8. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()`** (line 2143-2209)
   - Converts LoRA to GGML and uploads to HuggingFace Hub

#### Generic Save Functions (for non-LLM models):
9. **`unsloth_generic_save()`** (line 2356-2410)
   - Handles models that don't fit standard LLM architecture
   - Works with custom model types (vision, text)

10. **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()`** (line 2413-2453)
11. **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()`** (line 2456-2499)

#### Special Purpose Functions:
12. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()`** (line 2502-2563)
    - Saves model with TorchAO quantization
    - Merges LoRA, applies TorchAO quantization, then saves

13. **`patch_saving_functions()`** (line 2569-2678)
    - Dynamically patches models to add save/export methods
    - Adds methods: `save_pretrained_merged`, `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, `push_to_hub_gguf`, `save_pretrained_ggml`, `push_to_hub_ggml`, `save_pretrained_torchao`

#### Utility Functions:
- `save_to_gguf_generic()` (line 2276) - Generic GGUF saving for any model
- `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (line 2129)
- `create_huggingface_repo()` (line 1456)
- `upload_to_huggingface()` (line 1495)
- `create_ollama_modelfile()` (line 1618)

## Unsloth Architecture: Method Exposure & Model Wrapping

I've thoroughly analyzed the unsloth codebase. Here's a detailed breakdown of how methods are exposed on model objects:

---

## 1. METHOD ATTACHMENT MECHANISM: MONKEY-PATCHING VIA `types.MethodType`

**Primary Pattern:** Unsloth uses explicit **monkey-patching** through Python's `types.MethodType` to attach methods dynamically.

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476691180-d0bbx3/unsloth/save.py` (lines 2569-2678)

### The `patch_saving_functions()` Function

This is the **central hub** for attaching save/export methods:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    # ... setup code ...
    
    # 1. Patches the standard push_to_hub method
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    
    # 2. Dynamically creates a wrapper function via exec()
    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
        # ... enhanced with unsloth tags and metadata ...
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
    
    # 3. Attaches new methods using types.MethodType
    model.original_push_to_hub = original_push_to_hub
    model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, model)
    
    # 4. Adds custom export methods:
    if not vision:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**Key Line Numbers:**
- **Method wrapping (push_to_hub):** lines 2575-2640
- **Dynamic attachment of methods:** lines 2642-2677

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476691180-d0bbx3/unsloth/models/loader.py` (line 107)

```python

        model_name = "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
        max_seq_length = 2048,
        dtype = None,
        load_in_4bit = True,
        # ... 40+ parameters ...

        # Returns model already patched

```

## 1. METHOD ATTACHMENT MECHANISM: MONKEY-PATCHING VIA `types.MethodType`

**Primary Pattern:** Unsloth uses explicit **monkey-patching** through Python's `types.MethodType` to attach methods dynamically.

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476691180-d0bbx3/unsloth/save.py` (lines 2569-2678)

### The `patch_saving_functions()` Function

This is the **central hub** for attaching save/export methods:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    # ... setup code ...
    
    # 1. Patches the standard push_to_hub method
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    
    # 2. Dynamically creates a wrapper function via exec()
    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
        # ... enhanced with unsloth tags and metadata ...
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
    
    # 3. Attaches new methods using types.MethodType
    model.original_push_to_hub = original_push_to_hub
    model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, model)
    
    # 4. Adds custom export methods:
    if not vision:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Thorough Exploration Report: LoRA Adapter Weights in Unsloth Codebase

Based on my comprehensive exploration of the unsloth codebase, here is the complete understanding of LoRA adapter weights structure and implementation:

---

### 1. **LoRA Layer Structure and Classes**

**Key Classes and Their Attributes:**

- **PEFT-Based LoRA Layers**: The codebase uses PEFT's (`peft.tuners.lora`) implementation:
  - `Peft_Linear` (from `peft.tuners.lora.Linear`)
  - `Peft_Linear4bit` (from `peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit`)
  
  These classes contain the following critical attributes:
  - `lora_A` (dict): Dictionary of linear layers mapping adapter name → `nn.Linear` module (A matrix)
  - `lora_B` (dict): Dictionary of linear layers mapping adapter name → `nn.Linear` module (B matrix)
  - `scaling` (dict): Dictionary mapping adapter name → scaling factor (computed as `lora_alpha / r`)
  - `lora_dropout` (dict): Dictionary mapping adapter name → dropout module
  - `active_adapters` (list): List of currently active adapter names
  - `use_dora` (dict): Dictionary indicating whether DoRA is used per adapter
  - `lora_magnitude_vector` (dict): Dictionary for DoRA magnitude vectors (when applicable)
  - `base_layer`: The underlying original linear layer (frozen during LoRA training)

**Matrix Dimensions:**
- `lora_A.weight` has shape `(r, in_features)` where `r` is the rank
- `lora_B.weight` has shape `(out_features, r)`
- The LoRA update formula: `output = base_output + (input @ A) @ B * scaling`

---

### 2. **LoRA Parameter Extraction Functions**

**Primary Function**: `get_lora_parameters()` (in `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`, lines 190-232)

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Returns: (weight, weight_quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scaling)
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Get active adapter
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default",))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Extract LoRA matrices
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Variant Function**: `get_lora_parameters_bias()` (lines 235-257) - same as above but also returns `bias`

---

### 3. **LoRA Configuration Storage and Access**

**LoRA Config Creation** (in `/unsloth/models/llama.py`, lines 2598-2617):

The `LoraConfig` from PEFT is created with these key parameters:
- `r` (int): Rank of the LoRA matrices
- `lora_alpha` (int): Scaling factor for LoRA updates
- `target_modules` (list[str]): Names of modules to apply LoRA to (e.g., `["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]`)
- `lora_dropout` (float): Dropout applied to LoRA inputs
- `bias` (str): Whether to add bias (typically `"none"`)
- `layers_to_transform` (Optional): Specific layers to transform
- `use_rslora` (bool): Whether to use rank-stabilized LoRA
- `init_lora_weights` (bool or str): Initialization strategy
- `loftq_config` (dict): Configuration for LoftQ initialization
- `modules_to_save` (Optional[list]): Additional modules to save (not LoRA)

**Config Access Pattern**:
```python
model.peft_config["default"]  # Access the main PEFT config
model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()  # Convert to dictionary
model.peft_config[active_adapter]  # Access config by adapter name
```

---

### 4. **LoRA-Specific Files and Code Locations**

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Fast LoRA forward/backward autograd functions (LoRA_MLP, LoRA_QKV, LoRA_W) |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Helper functions: `get_lora_parameters()`, `get_lora_parameters_bias()`, `matmul_lora()`, `fast_linear_forward()` |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | LoRA initialization via `FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` (lines 2266-2947) |
| `/unsloth/models/vision.py` | Vision model LoRA setup (similar pattern to llama.py) |
| `/unsloth/save.py` | LoRA merging and saving (`_merge_lora()` function, lines 159-187) |
| `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` | LoRA config validation functions |
| `/tests/utils/data_utils.py` | Test utilities for describing LoRA weights |

---

### 5. **Module Iteration Patterns**

**Pattern 1: Layer-by-layer LoRA checking** (from `/unsloth/models/llama.py`, lines 2822-2895):
```python
for idx, layer in enumerate(model.model.model.layers):
    # Check MLP LoRA
    gate_proj = mlp_module.gate_proj
    if hasattr(gate_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(up_proj, "lora_A"):
        # MLP LoRA is enabled
        mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)
    
    # Check QKV LoRA
    q_proj = layer.self_attn.q_proj
    if hasattr(q_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(k_proj, "lora_A"):
        # QKV LoRA is enabled
        layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv
```

**Pattern 2: Full module iteration** (from `/unsloth/save.py`, lines 567-594):
```python
for j, layer in enumerate(internal_model.model.layers):
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:  # e.g., ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", ...]
        proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)
        # Merge LoRA: W = W + A.T @ (s * B.T)
```

**Pattern 3: Active adapter iteration** (from `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`, lines 487-515):
```python
for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        continue
    lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
    lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
    scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
    # Apply LoRA update
```

---

### 6. **LoRA Metadata Tracked Per Layer**

Each LoRA-enabled layer tracks:

| Metadata | Storage | Type | Purpose |
|----------|---------|------|---------|
| **Rank (r)** | `LoraConfig.r` | int | Dimensionality of A and B matrices |
| **Alpha** | `LoraConfig.lora_alpha` | int | Scaling multiplier for updates |
| **Scaling factor** | `proj.scaling[adapter]` | float | Computed as `lora_alpha / r` |
| **Target module names** | `LoraConfig.target_modules` | list[str] | Which module types have LoRA |
| **Active adapters** | `proj.active_adapters` | list[str] | Currently active adapter names |
| **Dropout** | `proj.lora_dropout[adapter]` | nn.Module | Applied to LoRA inputs |
| **DoRA enabled** | `proj.use_dora[adapter]` | bool | Whether to use DoRA variant |
| **Magnitude vectors** | `proj.lora_magnitude_vector[adapter]` | Optional[nn.Module] | DoRA magnitude vectors |
| **Base layer** | `proj.base_layer` | nn.Module | Original frozen linear layer |

---

### 7. **LoRA Merging Process**

**Core merging function** (from `/unsloth/save.py`, lines 159-187):

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    # Dequantize if needed
    if quant_state is not None:
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    # Merge: W = W + A.T @ (s * B.T)
    if A is not None:
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

**Full model merge flow**:
1. Iterate through all model layers
2. For each target module (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj, o_proj)
3. Call `_merge_lora()` to combine base weights with LoRA updates
4. Save merged weights to state dict

---

### 8. **Key LoRA Forward/Backward Functions**

**Fast LoRA Classes** (from `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`):

1. **`LoRA_MLP`** (lines 28-174): Fused forward/backward for SwiGLU/GeGLU MLP layers
   - Combines gate, up, down projections with LoRA
   
2. **`LoRA_QKV`** (lines 224-363): Fused forward/backward for Q, K, V projections
   - Applies LoRA to all three attention projections simultaneously
   
3. **`LoRA_W`** (lines 381-457): Generic single weight matrix LoRA
   - Used for output projection (o_proj)

**Helper functions**:
- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` (lines 178-190): Applies LoRA to SwiGLU MLP
- `apply_lora_qkv()` (lines 366-378): Applies LoRA to QKV projections
- `apply_lora_o()` (lines 460-465): Applies LoRA to output projection
- `matmul_lora()` (lines 734-757): Generic matrix multiplication with LoRA update

**Core LoRA computation** (from `matmul_lora`):
```python
out = torch_matmul(X, W)  # Base layer output
if A is not None:
    A, B = A.t(), B.t()
    XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
    out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha=s)  # Add LoRA contribution
```

---

### 9. **LoRA Parameter Access in Forward Pass**

**Fast path detection** (from `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`, lines 487-498):
```python
if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
    active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        return self.base_layer(x)  # No LoRA, use base layer
    
    lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight  # Shape: (r, in_features)
    lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight  # Shape: (out_features, r)

for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:

### 10. **LoRA Initialization and Patching**

**Patching process** (from `/unsloth/models/llama.py`, lines 2661-2902):

1. Create `LoraConfig` with rank, alpha, target_modules, etc.
2. Call `_get_peft_model()` to apply LoRA to model
3. Fix PEFT auto_mapping: `fix_lora_auto_mapping(model)`
4. **Patch MLP layers** if conditions are met:
   - Check `hasattr(gate_proj, "lora_A")`
   - Replace forward with optimized `_apply_lora_mlp`
5. **Patch QKV attention** if conditions are met:
   - Check `hasattr(q_proj, "lora_A")`
   - Replace with optimized `apply_lora_qkv`
6. **Patch output projection** if conditions are met:
   - Check `hasattr(o_proj, "lora_A")`
   - Replace with optimized `apply_lora_o`

if  hasattr(q_proj, "lora_A") and \

    hasattr(v_proj, "lora_A") and \

```

target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                  "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
```

These correspond to:
- **Attention**: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj (query, key, value)
- **Attention output**: o_proj (output projection)
- **MLP**: gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj (for SwiGLU activation)

def get_peft_weights(model):
    is_lora_weight = lambda name: any(s in name for s in ["lora_A", "lora_B"])
    return {
        name: param for name, param in model.named_parameters() 
        if is_lora_weight(name)

def describe_peft_weights(model):

        yield name, describe_param(param)
```

## Summary

The unsloth codebase implements LoRA adapter training using PEFT's `LoRA` and `Linear4bit` classes. The architecture centers around:

1. **Storage**: `lora_A`, `lora_B`, `scaling` dicts per adapter
2. **Metadata**: `r` (rank), `lora_alpha`, `target_modules` in `LoraConfig`
3. **Access**: Via `active_adapters` list and dicts indexed by adapter name
4. **Merging**: Using `addmm_` for efficient in-place matrix addition
5. **Optimization**: Custom CUDA kernels for MLP, QKV, and generic layers
6. **Patching**: Replacing forward/backward during training for performance

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Thorough Exploration Report: LoRA Adapter Weights in Unsloth Codebase

Based on my comprehensive exploration of the unsloth codebase, here is the complete understanding of LoRA adapter weights structure and implementation:

---

### 1. **LoRA Layer Structure and Classes**

**Key Classes and Their Attributes:**

- **PEFT-Based LoRA Layers**: The codebase uses PEFT's (`peft.tuners.lora`) implementation:
  - `Peft_Linear` (from `peft.tuners.lora.Linear`)
  - `Peft_Linear4bit` (from `peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit`)
  
  These classes contain the following critical attributes:
  - `lora_A` (dict): Dictionary of linear layers mapping adapter name → `nn.Linear` module (A matrix)
  - `lora_B` (dict): Dictionary of linear layers mapping adapter name → `nn.Linear` module (B matrix)
  - `scaling` (dict): Dictionary mapping adapter name → scaling factor (computed as `lora_alpha / r`)
  - `lora_dropout` (dict): Dictionary mapping adapter name → dropout module
  - `active_adapters` (list): List of currently active adapter names
  - `use_dora` (dict): Dictionary indicating whether DoRA is used per adapter
  - `lora_magnitude_vector` (dict): Dictionary for DoRA magnitude vectors (when applicable)
  - `base_layer`: The underlying original linear layer (frozen during LoRA training)

**Matrix Dimensions:**
- `lora_A.weight` has shape `(r, in_features)` where `r` is the rank
- `lora_B.weight` has shape `(out_features, r)`
- The LoRA update formula: `output = base_output + (input @ A) @ B * scaling`

---

### 2. **LoRA Parameter Extraction Functions**

**Primary Function**: `get_lora_parameters()` (in `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`, lines 190-232)

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Returns: (weight, weight_quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scaling)
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Get active adapter
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default",))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Extract LoRA matrices
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Variant Function**: `get_lora_parameters_bias()` (lines 235-257) - same as above but also returns `bias`

---

### 3. **LoRA Configuration Storage and Access**

**LoRA Config Creation** (in `/unsloth/models/llama.py`, lines 2598-2617):

The `LoraConfig` from PEFT is created with these key parameters:
- `r` (int): Rank of the LoRA matrices
- `lora_alpha` (int): Scaling factor for LoRA updates
- `target_modules` (list[str]): Names of modules to apply LoRA to (e.g., `["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]`)
- `lora_dropout` (float): Dropout applied to LoRA inputs
- `bias` (str): Whether to add bias (typically `"none"`)
- `layers_to_transform` (Optional): Specific layers to transform
- `use_rslora` (bool): Whether to use rank-stabilized LoRA
- `init_lora_weights` (bool or str): Initialization strategy
- `loftq_config` (dict): Configuration for LoftQ initialization
- `modules_to_save` (Optional[list]): Additional modules to save (not LoRA)

**Config Access Pattern**:
```python
model.peft_config["default"]  # Access the main PEFT config
model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()  # Convert to dictionary
model.peft_config[active_adapter]  # Access config by adapter name
```

---

### 4. **LoRA-Specific Files and Code Locations**

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Fast LoRA forward/backward autograd functions (LoRA_MLP, LoRA_QKV, LoRA_W) |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Helper functions: `get_lora_parameters()`, `get_lora_parameters_bias()`, `matmul_lora()`, `fast_linear_forward()` |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | LoRA initialization via `FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` (lines 2266-2947) |
| `/unsloth/models/vision.py` | Vision model LoRA setup (similar pattern to llama.py) |
| `/unsloth/save.py` | LoRA merging and saving (`_merge_lora()` function, lines 159-187) |
| `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` | LoRA config validation functions |
| `/tests/utils/data_utils.py` | Test utilities for describing LoRA weights |

---

### 5. **Module Iteration Patterns**

**Pattern 1: Layer-by-layer LoRA checking** (from `/unsloth/models/llama.py`, lines 2822-2895):
```python
for idx, layer in enumerate(model.model.model.layers):
    # Check MLP LoRA
    gate_proj = mlp_module.gate_proj
    if hasattr(gate_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(up_proj, "lora_A"):
        # MLP LoRA is enabled
        mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)
    
    # Check QKV LoRA
    q_proj = layer.self_attn.q_proj
    if hasattr(q_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(k_proj, "lora_A"):
        # QKV LoRA is enabled
        layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv
```

**Pattern 2: Full module iteration** (from `/unsloth/save.py`, lines 567-594):
```python
for j, layer in enumerate(internal_model.model.layers):
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:  # e.g., ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", ...]
        proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)
        # Merge LoRA: W = W + A.T @ (s * B.T)
```

**Pattern 3: Active adapter iteration** (from `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`, lines 487-515):
```python
for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        continue
    lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
    lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
    scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
    # Apply LoRA update
```

---

### 6. **LoRA Metadata Tracked Per Layer**

Each LoRA-enabled layer tracks:

| Metadata | Storage | Type | Purpose |
|----------|---------|------|---------|
| **Rank (r)** | `LoraConfig.r` | int | Dimensionality of A and B matrices |
| **Alpha** | `LoraConfig.lora_alpha` | int | Scaling multiplier for updates |
| **Scaling factor** | `proj.scaling[adapter]` | float | Computed as `lora_alpha / r` |
| **Target module names** | `LoraConfig.target_modules` | list[str] | Which module types have LoRA |
| **Active adapters** | `proj.active_adapters` | list[str] | Currently active adapter names |
| **Dropout** | `proj.lora_dropout[adapter]` | nn.Module | Applied to LoRA inputs |
| **DoRA enabled** | `proj.use_dora[adapter]` | bool | Whether to use DoRA variant |
| **Magnitude vectors** | `proj.lora_magnitude_vector[adapter]` | Optional[nn.Module] | DoRA magnitude vectors |
| **Base layer** | `proj.base_layer` | nn.Module | Original frozen linear layer |

---

### 7. **LoRA Merging Process**

**Core merging function** (from `/unsloth/save.py`, lines 159-187):

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    # Dequantize if needed
    if quant_state is not None:
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    # Merge: W = W + A.T @ (s * B.T)
    if A is not None:
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

**Full model merge flow**:
1. Iterate through all model layers
2. For each target module (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj, o_proj)
3. Call `_merge_lora()` to combine base weights with LoRA updates
4. Save merged weights to state dict

---

### 8. **Key LoRA Forward/Backward Functions**

**Fast LoRA Classes** (from `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`):

1. **`LoRA_MLP`** (lines 28-174): Fused forward/backward for SwiGLU/GeGLU MLP layers
   - Combines gate, up, down projections with LoRA
   
2. **`LoRA_QKV`** (lines 224-363): Fused forward/backward for Q, K, V projections
   - Applies LoRA to all three attention projections simultaneously
   
3. **`LoRA_W`** (lines 381-457): Generic single weight matrix LoRA
   - Used for output projection (o_proj)

**Helper functions**:
- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` (lines 178-190): Applies LoRA to SwiGLU MLP
- `apply_lora_qkv()` (lines 366-378): Applies LoRA to QKV projections
- `apply_lora_o()` (lines 460-465): Applies LoRA to output projection
- `matmul_lora()` (lines 734-757): Generic matrix multiplication with LoRA update

**Core LoRA computation** (from `matmul_lora`):
```python
out = torch_matmul(X, W)  # Base layer output
if A is not None:
    A, B = A.t(), B.t()
    XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
    out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha=s)  # Add LoRA contribution
```

# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")
